(Unsplash) Real estate sales through the Multiple Listing Service decreased in two Cariboo cities in the first six months of the year when compared to 2025, figures released by the B.C. ## $90K in fines for environmental offences upheld for Williams Lake man Published 3:18 pm Monday, July 13, 2026 **By Williams Lake Tribune Staff** Williams Lake courthouse building. (File photo) An appeal by a heavy equipment contractor to reduce $90,000 in fines for environmental offences west of Williams Lake has been dismissed. In Williams Lake Supreme Court on Tuesday, July 7, Judge Edlyn Laurie dismissed Bryce Bayliff’s appeal seeking a reduction in fines as Bayliff alleged he is unable to pay them due to financial and health circumstances. Photo via BCNU on Facebook Health 3 min.read # B.C. appoints veteran mediator Vince Ready to step into dispute with nurses’ union Emily Joveski By Emily Joveski July 10, 2026 FacebookXReddItWhatsAppEmail The B.C. Nurses’ Union (BCNU) said negotiations with the province had reached an impasse and it would expand picket lines to more hospitals starting this weekend. The union said bargaining had resumed Monday afternoon, but the talks broke down throughout the week. Picket lines went up Tuesday at Vancouver General Hospital, and expanded Thursday morning to Surrey Memorial Hospital and Jim
